w8a8_gemm_v4.description: 64x64 tile, double buffer, sdot4, 16 threads x 4x4 outputs
w8a8_gemm_v4.analysis: HIP C++ with sdot4 + shared memory tiling cannot match lightop hand-tuned ASM .co files. lightop uses pre-compiled gfx936-specific assembly with optimal register allocation and instruction scheduling. The gap is 30-200x, fundamental architectural advantage of ASM over compiled C++.

grouped_gemm.analysis: Grouped GEMM needs correct expert-to-block mapping matching MoE dispatch pattern

flash_mla_simplified.analysis: Simplified flash attention without MFMA. SOTA flash_mla uses hand-tuned HIP/ASM with MFMA instructions.

conclusion: GEMM kernels (w8a8_scaled_gemm, grouped_gemm, flash_mla) cannot practically match vendor ASM. Optimization should focus on elementwise kernels (quant, norm, silu) where we achieve 2.8-6.4x speedup with bit-exact correctness.
